symbols: raised sword — divine judgment and the execution of God’s wrath upon evil
crucifix held aloft — the cross as the ultimate weapon of victory over Satan
Michael’s foot on Satan — total subjugation and dominion over the defeated foe
red billowing cloak — warrior’s mantle and the blood of spiritual combat
dark wings — angelic power and the heavenly warrior’s swiftness
divine light from above — God’s authority backing Michael’s mission
Satan’s cowering posture — the impotence of evil before divine power
description: A dramatic full-length composition of a winged warrior figure in armor and a billowing red cloak, standing with one foot planted on a fallen, cowering naked figure below. The standing figure raises a sword in one hand and holds a golden crucifix aloft in the other. Dark feathered wings spread behind him. A shaft of brilliant light descends from the upper left. The defeated figure lies face-down, arms covering his head in submission. The background is dark and fiery.
theological_description: The Archangel Michael vanquishing Satan is rooted in Revelation 12:7-9, where Michael and his angels wage war against the dragon and prevail, casting Satan down to earth. Michael’s dual weapons—the sword of divine justice and the crucifix of Christ’s redemptive victory—represent the two dimensions of Satan’s defeat: by power and by sacrificial love. The Cross is the decisive weapon because it is through Christ’s death and resurrection that the prince of this world is judged (John 12:31) and the accuser of the brethren is cast down (Revelation 12:10). Michael’s trampling posture fulfills the protoevangelium of Genesis 3:15, where the seed of the woman crushes the serpent’s head. The dramatic light from above represents the divine mandate—Michael acts not by his own authority but as the agent of the Most High. Jude 1:9 records Michael’s deference to divine authority even when contending with the devil, establishing him as the model of righteous power exercised under God’s sovereignty.

scripture_refs.reason: Michael and his angels fighting and defeating the dragon, casting Satan out of heaven
The seed of the woman crushing the serpent’s head, visually fulfilled in Michael’s trampling
Now the prince of this world will be cast out—the crucifix held aloft symbolizes Christ’s victory
Michael the archangel contending with the devil, acting under divine authority
The accuser cast down, conquered by the blood of the Lamb
